evidence_excerpt: Nutrition improved and patient felt better after steroids and IV nutrition, but with residual issues including persistent anasarca/edema and need for ongoing anticoagulation and insulin titration: "With this your nutrition improved... we are happy that you're feeling better!" and discharge exam noted "persistent ... pitting edema... persistent anasarca." Ongoing Lovenox for DVT and diuretic started.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent swelling/anasarca at discharge: "some persistent ... pitting edema... persistent anasarca" and started on a diuretic; ongoing anticoagulation and insulin dose titration anticipated.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: "With this your nutrition improved." and "we are happy that you're feeling better!" after initiation of steroids and supplemental IV nutrition during hospitalization.
Earlier noted "He ambulates minimally"; at discharge: "Activity Status: Ambulatory - Independent."

"found to have a blood clot associated with the special IV line we placed while you were here. For this, we have started you on a blood thinner called Lovenox (Enoxaparin) which you will continue on for at least 3 months."

notes_on_uncertainty: No follow-up beyond hospital discharge is provided; long-term outcome and quality-of-life categories are inferred from discharge status within the same hospitalization. Persistent edema and ongoing treatment needs support partial recovery rather than complete recovery, but the true long-term trajectory is not documented.
